verdict.answer_first: A is less bad: sentence 1 says "I'll lay out a plan" while B opens with permission noise, "recall lookup... got blocked"; neither leads with the outcome.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits harder with "Managed SaaS first, free tiers, expand later"; A keeps branching with "Better Stack (or UptimeRobot)" and later asks what to prioritize.
verdict.fabrication: B invents more as fact: "PM2/launchd bridge, docbroker, agent fleet" and "session-log/recall system". A also invents stack details but labels them "Assumptions".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: "Phase 1 — Stop flying blind (Week 1)" plus concrete tools, checks, and "/health endpoint"; B is useful but starts with irrelevant blockage and spreads scope.
